name: Whispers of the Seasons
short_description: You have been marked by a monster to be its next prey. Defend your cabin against the beast across the four seasons, listening out for its attacks to stop it before it gets inside. Complete tasks throughout the night in an effort to free yourself from its curse and return to a normal life.
detailed_description:
You have been marked by a monster to be its next prey. Defend your cabin against the beast across the four seasons, listening out for its attacks to stop it before it gets inside. Complete tasks throughout the night in an effort to free yourself from its curse and return to a normal life.

This game uses spatial audio as a core part of its gameplay. Headphones with or speakers with stereo capability are highly recommended. 

Listen Carefully - The beast will try to get in your cabin by whispering a spell at your salt line protected windows. Listen out for its whispers to stop it before it gets the window open. If the wind from the open window manages to get rid of your salt line, there will be no saving you. The beast will try to distract you or cover up its sound, so being aware of your surroundings is crucial for your survival.

Defend Your Home Across The Four Seasons - Each season brings a new challenge to further test your awareness. From strong winds to full on thunderstorms, you'll have to be very perceptive in order to make sure you have all of your windows covered from the beast. Survive until the sun rises and you can consider yourself safe until the next seasonal solstice or equinox.

Complete Your Tasks - A local priest has answered the call to help you get rid of the beast for good. Follow his instructions for each night to prepare a ritual to trap the beast, or choose to go it alone and focus on your survival through the night.

reviews.review_text: I decided to try the game because of the overwhelmingly positive reviews, and long story short — it’s just a typical student-made project. You simply have to survive in a house by lighting candles, turning off devices that switch on by themselves, and keeping a monster from entering through the window just by holding a cross in your hand. The game clearly lacks mechanics — it feels very raw and suffocating.

I played for roughly 2 hours, and it could definitely be beaten in a shorter time if you are smart, but I enjoyed every bit of it. It's Tense and it keeps you super focused. Enough that my birds screaming or a text message genuinely made me jump.

I was super confused at first and definitely died a couple times the first night, but it does come pretty intuitively. I never found the safe code or finished all the side missions in the 2nd night. So there is definitely more that I haven't seen, in fact the events are random enough that I was experiencing new things on the final night and had things happen once that never happened again. Enough to keep it fresh the entire first run through and probably enough for multiple more. I never could find the grandfather clock noise though so every night had me trying to tone it out as I rushed around, but that is entirely on me being super unperceptive. The events are also really good at keep the fear going, a randomly moved table during the 3rd night impeded my 'safe' route and instantly threw me back into scrambling around. A nice balance of as the nights progress having less room for error but not feeling unfair, just enough that you don't ever feel too comfortable.

I am pretty sure the game runs on Godot, and it runs really well and has a really nice charm to the visuals. Somehow I never encountered any bugs besides some slight light clipping or weather phasing a bit in. Nothing that affected gameplay. The little details like the notebook changing when it's too late are also a great touch to show just how much passion went into it all. And of course, having an FoV slider going above my go to of 110 is something that so many skip out on, it's so appreciated.

Well worth playing, very fun and very unique. Headphones are definitely recommended though considering just how important directional audio is.

As a guy who normally does not play or enjoy horror games, I have to say this game is amazing. I absolutely love the theme and you can tell a lot of hard work and love went into this game. I highly recommend playing through this game, you will not be disappointed.

Really fun game! I had a great time playing it. It made me jump a few times, and the task you need to do will keep you busy while you're trying not to die from what I like to call "the demon deer" lol. overall its a short but very entertaining game.
big problem i crash every time i try to start the game after main menu
